---
title: "Mine Waste in the U.S."
subtitle: "A Geological Analysis using USGS NMWI Data"
author: "Indigo Sterud"
institute: "University of Illinois at Chicago"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: xaringan-themer.css
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      ratio: "16:9"
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
---

```{r load-packages, include = FALSE}
# Add any additional packages you need to this chunk
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(knitr)
library(xaringanthemer)
library(readr)
library(kableExtra)
library(purrr)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# For better figure resolution
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.retina = 3, dpi = 300, fig.width = 6, fig.asp = 0.618, out.width = "80%")
```

```{r load-data, include=FALSE}
Waste_Geology <- read_csv("/cloud/project/project-starter/data/Waste_Geology.csv")
Waste_Resources <- read_csv("/cloud/project/project-starter/data/Waste_Resources.csv")

mining_df <- left_join(Waste_Resources, Waste_Geology, by = "Site_ID") %>%
  rename(
    Site_Name = Site_Name.x,
    Site_Name_geo = Site_Name.y)

mining_clean <- mining_df %>%
  mutate(
    state_abbr = substr(Site_ID, 1, 2),
    state_name = state.name[match(state_abbr, state.abb)],
    log_vol = log10(Calc_Vol + 1)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(Calc_Vol, Ore_Min)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE}
style_xaringan(
  title_slide_background_image = "img/Innovation-in-mining.jpg"
)
```


## Research Questions

- Are there differences in waste production across states?

--

- Do certain mineral commodities produce more waste?

---

## Mining Data
- Columns represent different mine waste features for each Mine Location.
- Commodity = Minerals mine factories want
- Gangue_Min = non-commodity minerals

```{r glimpse-table, echo = FALSE}
mining_clean %>%
  select(Site_Name, state_abbr, Calc_Vol, Commodity, Gangue_Min) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  kable(format = "html") %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    font_size = 10
  )
```

---

class: center, middle

## Differences in Calculating Mining Waste per State

---

Arizona and Utah have large outliers...

- This reveals that using medians instead of means will be more beneficial in preventing skewed data.

```{r state-waste-regular, echo = FALSE}
ggplot(mining_clean, aes(x = reorder(state_abbr, Calc_Vol), y = Calc_Vol)) + 
  geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue") + 
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) + 
  labs( title = "Mine Waste Volume Distribution by State", x = "State", y = "Waste Volume (m^3)" )
```

---
##Using a log scale? Yes. Much Better.

```{r state-waste-log-plot, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
ggplot(mining_clean, aes(x = reorder(state_abbr, Calc_Vol),y = Calc_Vol)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "pink") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  labs( title = "Mine Waste Volume by State (Log Scale)", x = "State", y = "Waste Volume(m^3)")
```

---
## Other notes on mining data

1.) It is a giant compliaton of PUBLIC mining data across the US, compiled by USGS.

2.) Does not represent the whole of mines across each state / resource, or private companies.

3.) This already gives the data a lot of room for error.

---
Bootstrapping helps to normalize sample amounts since some states have less mine data than others.
```{r state-waste-bootstrap, Warning = FALSE, echo = FALSE}
set.seed(100)
bootstrap_state <- mining_clean %>%
  group_by(state_abbr) %>%
  summarize(
    boot_median = list(
      replicate(1000,
        median(sample(Calc_Vol, replace = TRUE), na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    )
  )

bootstrap_ci <- bootstrap_state %>%
  mutate(
    median_est = map_dbl(boot_median, median),
    lower = map_dbl(boot_median, ~quantile(.x, 0.025)),
    upper = map_dbl(boot_median, ~quantile(.x, 0.975))
  )

ggplot(bootstrap_ci,
       aes(x = reorder(state_abbr, median_est),
           y = median_est)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.35) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Bootstrapped Median Mine Waste Volume by State",
    x = "State",
    y = "Median Volume"
  )

```

---
class: inverse, middle, center

## Commodity Minerals and Waste

- What is the relationship between the type of mineral and the amount of waste produced?

---
```{r commodity-data, echo=FALSE}

Commodity_Long <- mining_clean %>%
  separate_rows(Commodity, sep = ",|;") %>%
  mutate(
    Commodity = trimws(Commodity),
    Commodity = gsub("\\s*\\(.*\\)", "", Commodity)
  )

Top20 <- Commodity_Long %>%
  group_by(Commodity) %>%
  summarize(median_vol = median(Calc_Vol, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  slice_max(median_vol, n = 20)

```

```{r commodity-plot, echo=FALSE}

ggplot(Top20, aes(x = reorder(Commodity, median_vol), y = median_vol)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkgreen") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "serif")) +
  labs(
    title = "Median Waste amt. where Commodity Minerals were Found",
    x = "Commodity Mineral",
    y = "Median Volume (m^3)"
  )

```

- Tellurium is often harvested from large Mine Waste Locations

- Gold is often associated with mines that are smaller scale and produce less waste.

---
# Commodity Scatterplot

*Silver, Molybdenum, Gold and Copper* are the most prevalent.

```{r commodity-scatter, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

Commodity_Filtered <- Commodity_Long %>%
  filter(Commodity %in% Top20$Commodity, Calc_Vol > 0)

ggplot(Commodity_Filtered, aes(x = Commodity, y = Calc_Vol, color = Commodity)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.6, width = 0.2) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "serif"),
        legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Mine Waste Volume by Commodity Mineral (Individual Sites)",
    x = "Commodity Mineral",
    y = "Waste Volume (m^3)"
  )

```
---

- The most amount of mine locations contained copper.

- The least amount of mine locations contained silver.

- All had roughly the same shape, probably meaning they were found alot of the same sites.

- All had roughly the same median amount of waste volume at the sites they were harvested (1E07)

```{r commodity-hist, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
ggplot(
  Commodity_Long %>%
    mutate(Commodity = trimws(Commodity)) %>%
    filter(Commodity %in% c("gold", "copper", "molybdenum", "silver"), Calc_Vol > 0),
  aes(x = log10(Calc_Vol), fill = Commodity)) +
  geom_histogram(alpha = 0.35, bins = 15) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "gold" = "#E69F00",
    "copper" = "#D55E00",
    "silver" = "grey",
    "molybdenum" = "steelblue")) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Mine Waste Volume by Commodity",
    x = "log10(Waste Volume (m^3))",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Commodity")

```
---
## ANOVA test for Commodity Minerals

- What does this mean??

```{r anova-commodity, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
anova_model <- aov(
  log10(Calc_Vol) ~ Commodity,
  data = Commodity_Long %>%
    filter(Commodity %in% Top20$Commodity, Calc_Vol > 0)
)

summary(anova_model)
```

---
class: inverse, middle, center

# Gangue Minerals

- What "economically undesireable" minerals are observed in high-waste sites?

---

- Stilbite and Apophyllite are the most common Gangue Minerals identified from high waste volume sites.

```{r gangue-plot, echo=FALSE}
Gangue_Plot_Data <- mining_clean %>%
  separate_rows(Gangue_Min, sep = ",|;") %>%
  mutate(Gangue_Min = trimws(Gangue_Min)) %>%
  mutate(Gangue_Min = gsub("\\s*\\(.*\\)", "", Gangue_Min)) %>%
  group_by(Gangue_Min) %>%
  summarize(mean_vol = mean(Calc_Vol, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  slice_max(mean_vol, n = 15)

Gangue_Plot <- Gangue_Plot_Data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Gangue_Min, mean_vol), y = mean_vol)) +
  geom_col(fill = "brown") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "serif", size = 12)) +
  labs(
    title = "Size of Mine and Gangue Minerals Identified",
    x = "Gangue Mineral",
    y = "Mean Volume"
  )

Gangue_Plot
```

---

# Stillbite

```{r stillbite, echo = FALSE, out.width = "60%", fig.align = "center", fig.cap = "Image credit: Photo by Enjoy Illinois."}
include_graphics("img/chicago-fall.jpeg")
```

---

# Images

```{r castle, echo = FALSE, out.width = "60%", fig.align = "center", fig.cap = "Image credit: Photo by Enjoy Illinois."}
include_graphics("img/chicago-fall.jpeg")
```


---

# Feeling adventurous?

- Want to find out more about `xaringan`? See https://slides.yihui.name/xaringan/#1.

- You are welcomed to use the default styling of the slides. In fact, that's what I expect majority of you will do. You will differentiate yourself with the content of your presentation.

- But some of you might want to play around with slide styling. The 
`xaringanthemer` provides some solutions for this that: https://pkg.garrickadenbuie.com/xaringanthemer.

- And if you want more bells and whistles, there is also `xaringanExtra`: https://pkg.garrickadenbuie.com/xaringanExtra.
